tender.title: 07-17-19-1-1040 Social Housing Property Repairs and Improvement Contract
tender.description: Barnsley Council is procuring a contract for housing repairs, servicing and capital improvements to one third of its social housing stock across the Borough. The successful tenderer will work alongside Berneslai Homes Construction Services (the Council's ALMO) who is appointed direct to deliver the remaining two-thirds of the work. Barnsley Council is committed to its services being best in class. An efficient and effective housing repair, servicing and capital improvements service across the Borough is a top priority, and one of the keystones of Council policy. The Council intends to maintain and improve upon a strong history of public:private sector partnership working to make the most of resources. That includes implementing a new contract for the combined provision of housing repairs, servicing and capital improvements to the social housing stock across the Borough. The current contract ends 31st March 2020, and the procurement for the new contract maintains the current model, namely two thirds of the work to be allocated to Berneslai Homes Construction Services (BHCS), and one third to be competitively tendered in the open market. The Council's four-year delivery plan for "Making best use of and improving existing housing stock" involves: - Continuing the sustainable approach to maintaining council housing - Helping social tenants move to more suitable accommodation that meets their needs - Improving energy efficiency of existing housing Council housing priorities include: - Maintaining 100% of the council stock to Barnsley Homes Standard - Improving the range of housing services online, and supporting tenants to get online - A sustainable financial model and cost plan Over the next few years, a significant number of skilled jobs can be created and supported as a result of the Social Housing Property Repairs and Improvement contract, and the delivery of this programme of work should incorporate approaches and processes that enable employment opportunities in the construction sector.

additionalClassifications.description: Ancillary works for pipelines and cables

tender.title: Planned Refurbishment Programmes of Works to Social Housing Properties within Blackpool (BCH)
tender.description:
Blackpool Coastal Housing (BCH) is Blackpool Council's agent for the management of the planned maintenance programmes on the social housing stock.  This includes building works necessary to keep the stock in good condition and structurally sound.  Work typically comprises larger scale refurbishment programmes including re-pointing, rendering, windows and doors replacements, fencing, replacement rainwater goods, fascias and soffits and asbestos removal. Roofing is not included in this tender.

There are approximately 4,900 properties within the social housing stock comprising 2,500 flats & and 2,400 houses.  Houses are located across Blackpool and flats are, in the main, grouped together in sites.

The work is divided into three lots as follows. Contractors may tender with the aim of being appointed to 1 or more lots.
Lot 1 - General Building Works
Lot 2 - Replacement of Windows & Doors. In addition to planned refurbishment works the successful contractor/s will also be required to undertake responsive window and door repair and replacement works including pre-measuring glass sizes, glazing single glazed and DGU's. 
Lot 3 - Fencing

tender.title: Eden District Council Housing Allocation and Homelessness Services
tender.description:
Eden District Council (EDC) sought bids from bidders for the administration of its housing allocation and homelessness functions covering the below key areas:
- Admin of the EDC housing advice function
- Admin of the EDC homelessness function
- Management of the EDC housing register

The service is required to be delivered throughout Eden. It will be a requirement of the successful bidder to make available a min of 6 units of temporary accommodation within Penrith, covering the following property types:
- 2x 2 or 3 bedroom family houses
- 4x 1 or 2 bed flats

The contract was initially tendered via an OJEU compliant Open tender procedure (under contract notice 2019/S 199-483730). One tender submission was received as part of the tender process. This tender process was however cancelled, since the costs submitted were substantially higher than the budget available to EDC.
tender.procurementMethodDetails: Negotiated procedure without prior publication (above threshold)
